recap: Egypt advanced to the round of 16 after defeating Australia 4-2 on penalties following a 1-1 draw, with Hossam Abdelmaguid converting the decisive spot kick after Australia's Mathew Ryan failed to save any of Egypt's four shots. Emam Ashour had put Egypt ahead in the 13th minute, but Mohamed Hany's own goal in the 55th leveled the match and made him the first player to score two own-goals in a single World Cup. Mohamed Salah, playing through a hamstring injury, was among Egypt's successful penalty takers as Australia fell to 0-3 in knockout competition.
